title: ggen Validation Gates — Definition of Done
description: Seven validation gates that must pass before artifacts are emitted. These gates enforce the five-stage pipeline (μ₁–μ₅) constraints and prevent defective code generation.

gates.name: SHACL Validation
gates.description: RDF ontology shapes validation using SHACL constraints. Validates that input TTL ontologies conform to declared shapes before entering the pipeline.
gates.recovery: Fix TTL ontology to conform to shapes; re-run validation

diagnostic_codes.meaning: RDF triple violates a sh:NodeShape constraint
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Add missing required properties to RDF triples

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Property value has wrong datatype (sh:datatype)
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Cast value to correct datatype or adjust shape constraint

diagnostic_codes.meaning: String value does not match sh:pattern regex
diagnostic_codes.example: IRI does not match expected namespace pattern
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Correct the value or update the pattern constraint

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Property count violates sh:minCount or sh:maxCount
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Add or remove instances to match cardinality constraint

basic.description: SHACL shapes on core ontology triples only

strict.description: SHACL shapes on all RDF including imports and inferences

invariants: All SHACL shapes must load without parse error
Input TTL must parse as valid Turtle syntax
Shape focus nodes must exist in RDF graph
Constraint violations must enumerate affected subjects and violations

pass.ttl:
ex:MyClass rdf:type rdfs:Class .
ex:MyClass rdfs:label "My Class" .
pass.shape: :ClassShape a sh:NodeShape ; sh:targetClass rdfs:Class ; sh:property [ sh:path rdfs:label ; sh:minCount 1 ] .
pass.result: ✓ SHACL validation passed; TTL conforms to shape

fail_missing_property.shape: :ClassShape a sh:NodeShape ; sh:targetClass rdfs:Class ; sh:property [ sh:path rdfs:label ; sh:minCount 1 ] .

gates.name: Profile Enforcement (Strict Mode)
gates.description: Validates that generated artifacts and manifest conform to declared marketplace profile constraints. Enforces immutability and consistency rules for pack configuration.
gates.recovery: Update ggen.toml profile constraints or suppress via config

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Output artifacts violate declared profile constraints
diagnostic_codes.example: Published profile forbids any write access to generated files
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Change profile to Draft or remove read-only constraint

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Pack immutability constraint violated (published pack modified)
diagnostic_codes.example: Version unchanged but content hash changed
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Bump semantic version or revert changes

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Manifest schema version mismatch (declared vs actual)
diagnostic_codes.example: ggen.toml declares manifest_version=v2 but emits v1
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Regenerate manifest or update ggen.toml version declaration

basic.description: Profile checks disabled; violations emit warnings only

strict.description: All profile constraints enforced; violations halt emission

invariants: Published packs are immutable (version + content hash combination is unique)
Profile constraints must be declared in [validation] section of ggen.toml
Manifest version in ggen.toml must match actual emitted manifest
Profile state transitions must follow typestate rules (Draft → Published is one-way)

pass_draft.result: ✓ Profile enforcement passed; Draft profile allows modifications

pass_published_unchanged.result: ✓ Published pack unchanged; content_hash matches previous run

fail_strict_violation.result: ✗ GGEN-PROFILE-002: Published pack version unchanged but content_hash changed (def456 ≠ abc123)

gates.name: SPARQL Conformance Checks
gates.description: Validates SPARQL queries (SELECT, CONSTRUCT) against provision rules and template binding contracts. Ensures all variables consumed by templates are produced by queries.
gates.law_surface: SPARQL Query ↔ Tera Template ↔ ggen.toml Output Pattern
gates.recovery: Fix SPARQL query, Tera template, or ggen.toml pattern to resolve unbound variables

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Template consumes {{ var }} that SELECT does not project
diagnostic_codes.example: Template: {{ person_name }}, but SELECT only projects ?personId
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Add ?person_name to SPARQL SELECT, or remove {{ person_name }} from template

diagnostic_codes.meaning: output_file pattern consumes unbound variable
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Add ?module binding to SPARQL, or replace {{ module }} with constant in ggen.toml

diagnostic_codes.meaning: CONSTRUCT without ORDER BY (non-deterministic iteration)
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Add ORDER BY to CONSTRUCT to ensure deterministic ordering; in Strict Mode this is ERROR

diagnostic_codes.meaning: SELECT without ORDER BY (non-deterministic result order)
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Add ORDER BY to SELECT; in Strict Mode this is ERROR

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Identity CONSTRUCT detected (template ≡ pattern, no-op mapping)
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Modify CONSTRUCT template to perform meaningful transformation, or use COPY instead

diagnostic_codes.meaning: SELECT * used (disables provision checks for individual variables)
diagnostic_codes.example: SELECT * WHERE { ?s ?p ?o } (cannot verify which vars are bound)
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Replace SELECT * with explicit SELECT ?s ?p ?o

basic.description: E0011/E0013 are warnings; GGEN-* codes halt
basic.action_on_fail: halt for GGEN-*, warn for E-codes

strict.description: E0011/E0013 promoted to errors; all codes halt

invariants: All {{ var }} in templates must exist as ?var in SPARQL SELECT
All {{var}} in output_file patterns must exist as ?var in SPARQL SELECT
CONSTRUCT and SELECT must have ORDER BY (determinism)
CONSTRUCT template must not be identical to WHERE clause (no-op detection)
SELECT * forbidden; must use explicit variable list

pass_bound_variables.result: ✓ GGEN-TPL-001 check passed; all template vars are in SELECT projection

fail_unbound_template_var.result: ✗ GGEN-TPL-001: Template consumes {{ person_name }} not in SELECT projection

fail_unbound_output_var.result: ✗ GGEN-OUT-001: output_file pattern consumes {{module}} not in SELECT projection

pass_construct_with_order.sparql: CONSTRUCT { ?s rdf:type ?type } WHERE { ?s rdf:type ?type } ORDER BY ?s
pass_construct_with_order.result: ✓ E0011 check passed; CONSTRUCT has ORDER BY

warn_construct_no_order.result: ⚠ E0011: CONSTRUCT lacks ORDER BY (determinism); in Strict Mode: ERROR

gates.name: Type-Level Validation (Rust Compiler)
gates.description: Enforces Rust type safety and lifetime constraints. Prevents unsafe code, unwrap/panic in production, and enforces Result<T,E> error handling everywhere.
gates.recovery: Fix type errors, remove unsafe blocks, replace unwrap/expect with Result handling

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Cannot find value in scope (undefined variable/function)
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Define the variable or import the function

diagnostic_codes.meaning: No method with that name (type mismatch or missing trait)
diagnostic_codes.example: &String does not have method `chars()` when String is expected
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Dereference the value or implement the required trait

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Value used after move (ownership violation)
diagnostic_codes.example: use of moved value `data` after calling `consume(data)`
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Clone the value before passing, or use reference instead of move

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Production code contains unsafe blocks outside of audit boundary
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Remove unsafe block or move to dedicated unsafe_audit module with // SAFETY docs

diagnostic_codes.meaning: unwrap()/expect()/panic!() in production code
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Replace with ? operator, match, or if let

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Fallible operation not wrapped in Result<T,E>
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Return Result: fn load_file() -> Result<String>

diagnostic_codes.meaning: todo!() left in production code
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Remove todo!() or replace with actual implementation

basic.description: Compiler errors halt; clippy warnings allowed in dev
basic.action_on_fail: halt on error, warn on clippy

strict.description: All clippy warnings treated as errors; no unsafe allowed

invariants: Zero compiler errors (E0xxx codes)
Zero unwrap/expect in production code (crates/*/src/)
Zero unsafe blocks outside audit boundaries
All fallible operations return Result<T, CrateError>
No todo!() in committed code
Clippy warnings must be addressed (no #[allow(...)] suppression without justification)

pass_error_handling.result: ✓ Type-level validation passed; error is Result

fail_unwrap.result: ✗ Production unwrap() detected; replace with ? or match

fail_missing_result.result: ✗ Fallible operation not wrapped in Result<T,E>

gates.name: Diagnostic Codes Resolution
gates.description: Verifies that all GGEN-* diagnostic codes raised by ggen-lsp have been resolved before artifact emission. Prevents diagnostic drift and ensures LSP analysis completeness.
gates.recovery: Fix all reported diagnostics; re-run ggen lsp check to verify clear

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Any GGEN-prefixed diagnostic code (GGEN-TPL-001, GGEN-OUT-001, etc.)
diagnostic_codes.note: See gates 1-3 for specific GGEN diagnostic codes
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Address the specific GGEN-* code according to its recovery procedure

basic.description: Active GGEN-* diagnostics collected but do not block sync

strict.description: Any active GGEN-* diagnostic blocks artifact emission

invariants: ggen-lsp analysis must complete without crashing or timeout
All GGEN-* codes must map to specific law surfaces (see gate-3 table)
Diagnostics must be path-addressable (file + line + column)
Diagnostic severity (ERROR/WARNING) must match declared severity in gate definitions
No GGEN-* diagnostic can be suppressed with #[allow(...)] (must be fixed)

pass_no_diagnostics.result: ✓ Diagnostic resolution passed; no GGEN-* codes active

pass_warnings_strict_off.result: ✓ Non-strict mode; E0011 warning does not block emission

fail_strict_mode_active.result: ✗ Strict mode active; GGEN-TPL-001 diagnostic blocks emission

gates.name: Output Layer Validation (Layer Violation Prevention)
gates.description: Validates that generated artifacts do not escape project boundaries. Prevents directory traversal attacks and enforces output file constraints.
gates.recovery: Remove or fix output_file patterns to stay within project root

diagnostic_codes.meaning: output_file pattern would produce path outside project root
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Use relative paths that remain within project root; remove ../ sequences

diagnostic_codes.meaning: output_file pattern attempts to overwrite protected system directory
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Restrict output to paths under project root

diagnostic_codes.meaning: output_file variable substitution produces unsafe path
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Sanitize variable values or restrict pattern to safe directories

basic.description: Paths must remain within project root; absolute paths forbidden

strict.description: Same as basic (no relaxation in strict mode)

automation.sync_entry: yes (μ₅ emit stage, before any file write)

invariants: output_file patterns must use only relative paths (no leading /)
output_file must resolve to a canonical path within project root
No ../ path traversal sequences allowed (symlink traversal blocked)
Project root must be determined from ggen.toml location or CLI flag
All variable substitutions must be validated against allowed character sets (no shell metacharacters)

pass_relative_path.result: ✓ GGEN-YIELD-001 passed; path within project root

fail_absolute_path.result: ✗ GGEN-YIELD-001: Absolute path /etc/passwd escapes project root

fail_traversal.result: ✗ GGEN-YIELD-001: Path traversal (../../) escapes project root

fail_unsafe_substitution.result: ✗ GGEN-YIELD-003: Variable substitution produces unsafe path

gates.name: Determinism Validation (Reproducible Builds)
gates.description: Validates that ggen sync is deterministic: same inputs produce identical artifact hashes across runs. Enforces hash verification of input TTL, queries, and templates.
gates.recovery: Investigate non-deterministic sources (timestamps, UUIDs, random number generators, file system order)

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Output artifact hash changed despite identical input hashes
diagnostic_codes.example: input_hash(spec.ttl) = abc123, but output file hash changed from def456 to ghi789
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Audit code for non-deterministic behavior (uuid::Uuid::new_v4(), SystemTime::now(), random(), unordered sets)

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Input hash missing or empty (not computed at pack install time)
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Re-run pack install to compute pack TOML digest; use --locked mode

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Receipt signature empty (not signed with Ed25519 key)
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Verify signing key exists and is valid; re-run ggen sync

diagnostic_codes.meaning: Output file ordering non-deterministic (CONSTRUCT/SELECT lacks ORDER BY)
diagnostic_codes.example: Iteration order of SPARQL results varies across runs
diagnostic_codes.recovery: Add ORDER BY to SPARQL query to enforce deterministic result ordering

basic.description: Determinism audit runs; violations logged but do not block emission

strict.description: Non-deterministic outputs halt emission; must have identical hash

invariants: Same input_hashes must produce identical output_hashes
BLAKE3 / SHA-256 hashes must be computed at file write time
Cryptographic receipt must bind input→output hash chain
No UUID, timestamp, or RNG calls in code generation logic
All SPARQL results must be ordered (ORDER BY mandatory for determinism)
File iteration (walk directory tree) must be sorted

pass_deterministic.result: ✓ GGEN-DETERM-001 passed; identical inputs → identical outputs

fail_non_deterministic.result: ✗ GGEN-DETERM-001: Same inputs but output hash changed (jkl012 → pqr678)

fail_missing_digest.result: ✗ GGEN-DETERM-002: Pack digest empty; lockfile invalid

fail_empty_signature.result: ✗ GGEN-DETERM-003: Receipt signature empty; cryptographic chain broken

command_sequence: ggen validate spec.ttl             # Gate 1: SHACL validation
ggen lsp check --all               # Gate 3, 5: SPARQL conformance + diagnostics
ggen sync --validate-only          # Gate 2, 6, 7: Profile, yield, determinism pre-check
ggen sync --audit true             # Gate 2, 6, 7: Full validation + receipt generation

gate_1_fails: 1. Check TTL syntax: ggen validate spec.ttl (shows parse errors)
2. Check SHACL shapes: ensure shapes.ttl exists and loads
3. Verify shape constraints match data: read violation messages carefully
4. Add required properties to RDF triples or adjust constraints
gate_2_fails: 2. If Published: verify version and content_hash match previous release
3. Bump version if content changed: [package] version = "1.0.1"
gate_3_fails: 1. Run: ggen lsp check --all (lists all SPARQL/template violations)
2. For GGEN-TPL-001: ensure all {{ vars }} are in SELECT projection
3. For GGEN-OUT-001: ensure all {{vars}} in output_file are in SELECT
4. For E0011/E0013: add ORDER BY to CONSTRUCT/SELECT queries
gate_4_fails: 1. Run: just check (shows all compiler errors)
3. Remove unwrap/expect: use ? operator or match
gate_5_fails: 1. Run: ggen lsp check --all (collects all active diagnostics)
2. For each GGEN-* code: follow recovery procedure in gate-3 table
3. Disable strict_mode to allow non-strict diagnostics through: strict_mode = false
4. Re-run: ggen lsp check --all to verify clear
gate_6_fails: 1. Check output_file patterns: ensure no leading / (absolute paths)
3. Verify all {{vars}} in output_file resolve to safe values
4. Run: ggen sync --validate-only (shows yield gate violations)
gate_7_fails: 1. Run ggen sync twice with identical inputs: compare output hashes
2. If hashes differ: search code for non-deterministic sources
3. Remove: Uuid::new_v4(), SystemTime::now(), random(), unordered collections
4. Add ORDER BY to all SPARQL queries
5. Verify lockfile digest populated: .ggen/packs.lock has non-empty digest fields

before_sync: [ ] Check .ggen/packs.lock exists and has non-empty digests
after_sync: [ ] Check input_hashes and output_hashes in receipt
[ ] Run ggen sync again: verify identical output hashes (determinism)
[ ] Review OTEL traces: pipeline.* spans present
